questions.q: The Brahmastra is named after and associated with which god?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is the divine missile attributed to the creator-god Brahma.

questions.q: Which hero loosed the Brahmastra to slay Ravana in the final battle?
questions.explanation: Rama discharged the Brahmastra that struck down Ravana.

questions.q: Where did the Brahmastra strike Ravana, ending his life?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra pierced Ravana through the heart, killing him.

questions.q: What kind of object is the Brahmastra?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is an irresistible divine missile invoked with mantras.

questions.q: How is the Brahmastra invoked before it is launched?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is invoked with sacred mantras before being released.

questions.q: The Brahmastra is best described as which type of weapon?
questions.explanation: Tradition describes the Brahmastra as an irresistible, almost unstoppable divine weapon.

questions.q: Whom did Rama defeat with the Brahmastra at Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama used the Brahmastra to defeat and kill Ravana, the demon-king of Lanka.

questions.q: In which kingdom did Rama's final duel using the Brahmastra take place?
questions.explanation: The climactic battle in which Rama loosed the Brahmastra was fought at Lanka.

questions.q: Rama, who wielded the Brahmastra, was a prince of which city?
questions.explanation: Rama was the prince of Ayodhya who ultimately slew Ravana.

questions.q: From what kind of arms did Rama launch the Brahmastra at Ravana?
questions.explanation: Rama was a great archer and discharged the Brahmastra from his bow.

questions.q: According to tradition, which sage instructed the young Rama in the secret astras including the Brahmastra?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra taught Rama the divine missiles (astras) during his early adventures.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the decisive arrow Rama used to slay Ravana is often said to have been given by which sage?
questions.explanation: The sage Agastya is associated with empowering and counselling Rama for the final blow against Ravana.

questions.q: What made the Brahmastra effective against Ravana when ordinary arrows had failed?
options: It was an unstoppable divine missile invoked with mantras
It was made of pure gold
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra was a supreme divine weapon whose power no ordinary defense could withstand.

questions.q: Why did Rama's earlier arrows fail to kill Ravana before he used the ultimate weapon?
options: Ravana could not be touched by metal
questions.explanation: Ravana's heads regenerated when cut, so a single supreme weapon to the heart was needed to end him.

questions.q: Which weapon class does the Brahmastra belong to in Hindu tradition?
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is an astra, a celestial weapon summoned and directed by mantras rather than a mere handheld arm.

questions.q: Who was Ravana's wife who mourned after he was struck down by Rama's ultimate weapon?
questions.explanation: Mandodari, Ravana's chief queen, lamented over his body after the Brahmastra slew him.

questions.q: Which brother of Ravana defected to Rama and witnessed the demon-king's fall?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana sided with Rama and was present at Ravana's defeat by the Brahmastra.

questions.q: The slaying of Ravana with the Brahmastra is the climax of which book (kanda) of the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Ravana's death occurs in the Yuddha Kanda, the war book of the Ramayana.

questions.q: Rama, the wielder of the Brahmastra, is traditionally regarded as an avatar of which god?
questions.explanation: Rama is venerated as the seventh avatar of Vishnu.

questions.q: What is the most famous property attributed to the Brahmastra in tradition?
options: It once launched, cannot be countered and is unfailing
It must be thrown by hand
questions.explanation: The Brahmastra is renowned as an unfailing weapon that finds its mark and cannot be repelled.

questions.q: Before the final blow, Rama was counselled in the secret of Ravana's life. What was the key to ending Ravana?
options: Striking him in the heart with a supreme weapon
questions.explanation: Rama was advised that only a supreme weapon driven into Ravana's heart could end him, which the Brahmastra accomplished.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, which divine architect is credited with fashioning the celestial weapons of the gods, such as the Brahmastra?
questions.explanation: Vishvakarma, the divine architect and smith, is traditionally credited with fashioning the celestial weapons of the gods.

questions.q: The Aditya Hridayam hymn, recited to Rama before he slew Ravana, was taught to him by which sage on the battlefield?
questions.explanation: The sage Agastya taught Rama the Aditya Hridayam hymn to the Sun just before the decisive fight.

questions.q: Counter-weapons existed for divine astras. Which other supreme astra is the Brahmastra most often paired or compared with as its equal in Hindu epic tradition?
questions.explanation: The Pashupatastra of Shiva is traditionally counted among the supreme astras alongside the Brahmastra.

questions.q: In tradition, the Brahmastra is said to be invoked correctly only by one with what qualification?
options: Knowledge of the proper mantras and self-discipline
questions.explanation: Only one who knew the precise mantras and had the spiritual discipline could summon and wield the Brahmastra.

questions.q: Ravana had earlier obtained boons that protected him from gods and demons but, in his pride, omitted protection from which beings, allowing Rama to slay him?
questions.explanation: Ravana's boon excluded humans and lesser beings, so the human Rama could kill him.

questions.q: After Ravana fell to the Brahmastra, who did Rama install as the new king of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama crowned Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother, as the king of Lanka.

questions.q: In the wider epic tradition, misuse of the Brahmastra was believed to cause what kind of catastrophe?
options: Drought, famine, and devastation of the land
questions.explanation: Tradition warns that the Brahmastra's misuse brought drought, famine, and ruin to the land for years.

questions.q: Rama's charioteer for the final battle, sent by Indra to aid him against Ravana, was named:
questions.explanation: Indra sent his own charioteer Matali with a celestial chariot to carry Rama in the duel with Ravana.

questions.q: Which celestial chariot, lent by Indra, did Rama ride when he loosed the Brahmastra at Ravana?
options: Indra's own divine chariot driven by Matali
questions.explanation: Rama fought from Indra's celestial chariot, driven by the charioteer Matali, when he slew Ravana.
